message:
test(status-partition): check in proptest regression seeds + sync beads

Commit the companion `tests/proptest_status_partition.proptest-regressions`
seed file for the newly-added TLP metamorphic test. The proptest crate
records seeds of failure cases it has shrunk so that later runs re-exercise
them before exploring new cases, and the file's own leading comment
recommends checking it into source control — which is why the repo already
has sibling `tests/proptest_{id,jsonl_roundtrip,merge}.proptest-regressions`
files under version control.

Seeds recorded so far:
- `[Tombstone, Open]` with priorities `[Priority(0), Priority(0)]` and
  types `[Task, Task]` — shrunk from a larger failure case exercising
  closed-equivalent partitioning.
- `[Open, Open, Open, Open, Open]` with the same minimal priority/type
  shape — the all-same-status degenerate case where the union and
  no-filter queries must still be identical.

Regression coverage is committed so future proptest runs validate these
seeds up front before generating new ones; losing them would leak past
bug coverage into the test oracle's random-search budget.

Also bumps `.beads/issues.jsonl` by one line from `br sync --flush-only`
after the proptest landing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,8 @@
+# Seeds for failure cases proptest has generated in the past. It is
+# automatically read and these particular cases re-run before any
+# novel cases are generated.
+#
+# It is recommended to check this file in to source control so that
+# everyone who runs the test benefits from these saved cases.
+cc d68ea2ca9ac781726cc933fac38d2ffce3985718554e20ce481b63e629fe1a67 # shrinks to statuses = [Tombstone, Open], priorities = [Priority(0), Priority(0)], types = [Task, Task]
+cc 8d9ba157719e57a2b1b3a8b3d1e8e884be6c522f41d7eed671da45db302c9115 # shrinks to statuses = [Open, Open, Open, Open, Open], priorities = [Priority(0), Priority(0)], types = [Task, Task]
